title: The One With The Home Study
weakest_section: 04:00-04:30 range with gym shirt and paleontologist material

jokes.text: I came up with a couple of cost-cutting solutions, wrote out a list and Monica told me to go to hell.
jokes.setup: Chandler presents himself as the practical problem-solver for wedding costs
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt rejection reveals the futility of his efforts
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven humor that establishes Monica's control and Chandler's resigned acceptance. Well-structured with good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about the wedding costs, and I have a solution. What if we cut the guest list in half?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What if we have it in a park instead of a venue?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, what if we just elope?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, Chandler.

jokes.text: I'm responsible for just like half of that
jokes.setup: Discussion of weddings being a 40-billion-dollar industry
jokes.punchline: Monica claims personal responsibility for half the entire industry
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's obsessive nature about weddings, but relatively straightforward execution.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I have to take responsibility for at least half of that.

jokes.text: Maybe you do that next time you get married
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike's charitable gesture is praised
jokes.punchline: Casual assumption that this marriage will fail
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about marriage failure delivered casually. Good economy but lacks character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We decided to donate all our wedding gifts to charity.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's so generous! You guys are amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, really beautiful. So, uh, when's the divorce?

jokes.text: No, no, no, the next time's going to be Hawaii at sunset.
jokes.setup: Previous joke about 'next time' getting married
jokes.punchline: Phoebe matter-of-factly plans her future divorce and remarriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her casual attitude toward marriage failure escalated with romantic planning. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm not worried. I'm just gonna marry him, then in like five years we'll get divorced, and then I'll marry someone else.

jokes.text: But maybe the time after that
jokes.setup: Already planning second marriage in Hawaii
jokes.punchline: Now considering a third marriage for charity
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that pushes the absurdity further. Perfect economy and pure Phoebe logic. One of the episode's strongest jokes.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm already planning my second marriage in Hawaii. Now I'm considering a third marriage for charity.

jokes.text: "We"?
jokes.setup: Monica says 'we're cleaning the apartment'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's incredulous reaction to being included
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment about relationship dynamics. Good economy but predictable setup.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're cleaning the apartment? Since when do I clean? I'm not even sure I know how to hold a sponge.

jokes.text: You can't have it both ways!
jokes.setup: Monica excludes him from cleaning but uses 'we'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical protest about the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Solid relationship humor showing Chandler's frustration with Monica's controlling nature.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We need to clean the apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We? You just told me to go to the store!

jokes.text: It's very impressive when the little people know a celebrity
jokes.setup: Joey offers to help with the adoption interview
jokes.punchline: His condescending view of non-celebrities
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey narcissism delivered with complete sincerity. Good character integration but somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, I got this. I've done a lot of acting, so I know how to convince people of things that aren't true.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You just gotta make them think you're something you're not. Like, you're not celebrities, but we can work around that.

jokes.text: "Little people"? "Celebrity"!
jokes.setup: Joey's condescending comment
jokes.punchline: Monica and Chandler react to different offensive parts
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing how each character focuses on different problems with Joey's statement.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did you just call me stupid?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no. I called you *naive*.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not better!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, but at least you'll understand what it means.

jokes.text: I don't and I'm offended by the insinuation
jokes.setup: Monica asks about hidden inappropriate items
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock indignation while clearly lying
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom setup for the upcoming revelation. Functional but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you have any hidden inappropriate magazines or videos?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Me? No! Why would I have those? I'm a respectable man. I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more offended right now?

jokes.text: Okay, so there's not a magazine under the couch or a pack of cigarettes taped to the back of the toilet tank. or a filthy video in the VCR
jokes.setup: Monica's seemingly innocent verification
jokes.punchline: Her suspiciously specific knowledge of hiding places
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list that reveals Monica's thorough knowledge of Chandler's hiding spots.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just asking where you'd hide something... hypothetically. Like, not in the obvious places—not under the mattress, not in the closet, not behind the books on the shelf. Maybe somewhere like... behind the radiator? Or taped to the back of a drawer? Or inside a hollowed-out book?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, why are you asking me this?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not! I'm just curious where *you* would hide something.

jokes.text: I admit to the cigarettes and the magazine but that tape is not mine
jokes.setup: Monica's specific accusations
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits to most but draws the line at the video
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler's priorities in what he'll admit to versus what's too embarrassing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You bought a video?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, okay? I bought a video. But I want you to know that I bought it for an entirely different reason than you think.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What reason?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wanted to see what all the fuss was about... for educational purposes!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Educational purposes?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. Okay, fine. I'm... I'm aware of the issue.

jokes.text: I guess we'll never know whose it is
jokes.setup: Both deny ownership of the video
jokes.punchline: Their mutual agreement to let it remain a mystery
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan ending to the sequence that acknowledges the absurdity of their mutual denial.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I didn't make that video.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't make it either.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So we're just going to pretend this never happened?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I think that's for the best.

jokes.text: Well, I think you're going to appreciate the crap out of this one
jokes.setup: Charity worker mentions any contribution is appreciated
jokes.punchline: Mike's crude rephrasing of 'appreciate'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that doesn't particularly fit Mike's character. Somewhat lazy humor.

jokes.text: Completely anonymous. From two kind strangers Mr. X and Phoebe Buffay
jokes.setup: They claim to want complete anonymity
jokes.punchline: Immediately provide their real names
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing their inability to actually be anonymous, very much in character for both.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I want complete anonymity.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Me too, I don't want anyone to know who I am.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Mike.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm Phoebe Buffay.

jokes.text: Not necessary - Buffay is spelled B-u-f-f-a-y
jokes.setup: Mike says recognition isn't necessary
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately provides spelling for their 'anonymous' donation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - she can't resist making sure her name is spelled correctly even while being 'anonymous'.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Recognition isn't necessary.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh good, because we'd like to donate anonymously. That's P-H-O-E-B-E and M-I-K-E.

jokes.text: And X is spelled Mike Hannigan
jokes.setup: Previous spelling clarification
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic about how to spell 'X'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic that's both absurd and somehow makes sense in her world. Excellent economy and pure character comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, look, we get this free T-shirt. Uh, actually that's the shirt I wore to the gym
jokes.setup: Mike excited about getting a free T-shirt
jokes.punchline: It's actually just the charity worker's used gym shirt
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but lacks character specificity and feels somewhat random.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Wow, a free T-shirt! This is amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's a great shirt. Barely worn.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: This is perfect!

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: Well, it's actually my gym shirt. I figured I'd donate it since I upgraded to a new one.

jokes.text: Um, it's moist
jokes.setup: Revelation that the 'free' shirt is actually used
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable detail about its condition
jokes.explanation: Relies on grossness for humor rather than wit. Economy is good but feels cheap.

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: Well, it's not exactly free. It's a used shirt.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Used? How used?

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: Let's just say the previous owner had a very active lifestyle. Mostly running. From the police.

jokes.text: There was one! She's it! All the rest look like they should live under a bridge!
jokes.setup: Ross tries to be optimistic about other paleontologists
jokes.punchline: His immediate desperate admission and cruel description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's desperation and tendency toward inappropriate comments when stressed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, you know, there are a lot of talented paleontologists out there doing great work.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But let's be honest, they're all idiots.

jokes.text: Like I said, I was thinking of taking Emma to the museum of knives and fire.
jokes.setup: Rachel's horrified reaction to playground suggestion
jokes.punchline: Ross's absurd alternative that's clearly more dangerous
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor that highlights Rachel's overprotectiveness by proposing something genuinely dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A playground? Ross, that's insane!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, what do you suggest? We could always go bungee jumping off the Brooklyn Bridge instead.

jokes.text: And it was uneven for weeks
jokes.setup: Rachel's traumatic swing story about having hair cut
jokes.punchline: The 'trauma' was just uneven hair
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Rachel's vanity-driven 'trauma'. The understatement works well.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I was on this swing, and my hair got caught. It was so traumatic!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what happened?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: They had to cut it out. I had this huge chunk missing.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That sounds horrible!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It was. But honestly? It was just uneven hair.

jokes.text: And you made it 'through' that?
jokes.setup: Rachel's dramatic retelling of her hair trauma
jokes.punchline: Ross's mock amazement at her 'survival'
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that deflates Rachel's drama. Solid character interaction.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wow, so you're telling me you survived this traumatic hair experience? That's incredible. I mean, most people don't make it out of a bad haircut alive.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, it's not funny!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm serious. You should write a book. 'I Survived: A Haircut Story.' It'll be huge.

jokes.text: I wonder who's going to play you in the movie.
jokes.setup: Previous sarcastic comment about Rachel's 'trauma'
jokes.punchline: Suggesting her story is movie-worthy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the sarcasm that maintains the mockery while being creative.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What, you mean like a Lifetime movie? 'Rachel Green: A Story of Survival'? Coming this fall to a cable network near you.

jokes.text: And I was thinking Claire Danes
jokes.setup: Ross's sarcastic movie comment
jokes.punchline: Rachel seriously considers casting for her 'trauma' movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel moment - completely missing Ross's sarcasm and getting genuinely excited about the idea.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, a movie about my trauma? That's actually genius. Who would play me?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It has to be someone who can really capture my vulnerability and... my hair.

jokes.text: Ross, those things go like 40 miles an hour, okay? and there's this moment when you're at the top, you just don't know if you're going to return back to earth!
jokes.setup: Discussion of swing safety
jokes.punchline: Rachel's completely exaggerated description of swing physics
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist take on playground swings that shows Rachel's dramatic overreaction perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so like, when you're on a swing, you have to be SO careful because if you go too high, you literally become a projectile. Like, you could just... fly off into space. And then your hair gets all messed up from the velocity, and you're just floating there in the atmosphere wondering how you're going to get down.

jokes.text: Space 'is' filled with orbiting children
jokes.setup: Previous exaggerated swing physics
jokes.punchline: Escalation to cosmic consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation with perfect economy. The visual imagery is both ridiculous and memorable. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so if I swing any higher, what happens?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, at a certain point, you achieve escape velocity and just... drift into space.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's not funny!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Actually, it kind of is.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And then you become a new planet. 'Planet Rachel.' Very exclusive real estate.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Technically speaking, you'd need significantly more mass to—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, not now!

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. That's the same, I'm sure there are 30 different species of poisonous swings
jokes.setup: Ross compares her swing fear to his spider fear
jokes.punchline: Rachel's absurd biological classification of swings
jokes.explanation: Clever response that applies scientific terminology to playground equipment, maintaining the absurdist tone.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A swing is not a bug, Ross!

jokes.text: Hello, is this the creepy residence?
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler expect the adoption social worker
jokes.punchline: Visitor looking for someone named 'Creepy'
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection gag that provides tension relief but lacks character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: They're here! They're here!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. Just be yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I am myself.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, that's what I'm afraid of.

dialogue.speaker: Visitor
dialogue.line: Hi, we're looking for Creepy?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Creepy?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, they think our apartment number is a name!

jokes.text: I told you I am not coming to a naked wedding!
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she won't wear a dress
jokes.punchline: Monica's assumption about what kind of wedding it would be
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Monica's literal interpretation and concern about propriety.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm not wearing a dress.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Dresses are a tool of the patriarchy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's my wedding!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, and it's going to be beautiful. I'm just gonna wear pants.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, are we having a lesbian wedding?

jokes.text: well, radiant!
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries on the veil despite claiming not to care about wedding appearance
jokes.punchline: Her immediate transformation and glowing reaction
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing the universal power of wedding attire to change a woman's mind.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't care about looking nice for the wedding. It's not important to me.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God! I look like a princess! I'm so beautiful!

jokes.text: You know, I was just there for jury duty. They've really spruced that place up
jokes.setup: Discussion of getting married at city hall
jokes.punchline: Monica's attempt to be supportive by referencing jury duty
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor that shows Monica trying to be encouraging while revealing the unromantic reality.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know, city hall marriages are very romantic. I mean, look at jury duty - people meet, they fall in love, they make a lifetime commitment.

jokes.text: That's way more important than some stupid kids
jokes.setup: Discussion of taking money back from charity for the wedding
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt dismissal of children's welfare for wedding purposes
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Monica's wedding obsession overriding her usual moral compass.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We're taking the money back from the children's hospital? What if a child needs a... a new liver?

jokes.text: That's sweet, honey but save something for the adoption lady
jokes.setup: Monica's harsh comment about kids
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about her attitude toward children
jokes.explanation: Excellent dry humor that points out the irony of Monica's anti-child sentiment while trying to adopt.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could you BE any more hostile toward children?

jokes.text: Rach, she's got like three hairs - I know, I know but they're just so beautiful
jokes.setup: Rachel worrying about Emma's hair on the swing
jokes.punchline: Her protectiveness over practically nonexistent hair
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's excessive protectiveness extending to minimal baby hair.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, her hair! Be careful with her hair!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, she's three years old. She has like five hairs.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Exactly! Five hairs! And I am not losing a single one!

jokes.text: Oh my God, I just pulled one out
jokes.setup: Rachel being overprotective of Emma's few hairs
jokes.punchline: She accidentally does the very thing she's trying to prevent
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback that shows Rachel creating the very trauma she fears.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Emma's hair is so delicate. I have to be so careful.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: One wrong move and it could all fall out.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm just going to gently...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh no! No no no no no!

jokes.text: We're seriously asking for the money back?
jokes.setup: They're at the charity to request their donation back
jokes.punchline: Mike's uncomfortable realization of how this looks
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment that acknowledges the moral awkwardness of their situation.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me you want your donation back?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, we need it for something else.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: So... you're taking money away from children with cancer.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: When you say it like that, it sounds bad.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I'm not a bad guy, but right now, I'm definitely looking like one.

jokes.text: Alright, now. Is this guy gay or straight? Cause one of us's going to have to start flirting
jokes.setup: They need to charm the charity worker to get their money back
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical approach to manipulation
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe moment showing her matter-of-fact approach to getting what she wants.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so we need to charm her. I'll use my wiles.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Your wiles?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm very wily. I once convinced a man to give me his car.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: How?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I told him it was a really nice car.

jokes.text: Gay, go!
jokes.setup: Previous plan to have someone flirt
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's quick tactical decision
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with excellent economy. Shows Phoebe's strategic mind in action.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, I'll do it. I'll flirt with him.

jokes.text: Oh my God! I love your shirt
jokes.setup: Phoebe tells Mike to flirt with the gay charity worker
jokes.punchline: Mike's awkward attempt at flirtation
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward flirtation humor. Functional but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Hey... do you come here often?

dialogue.speaker: Charity Worker
dialogue.line: It's a charity event. I work here.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Right, right. So... you ever think about... not working here?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Mike, stop.

jokes.text: This feels really good
jokes.setup: They're asking for charity money back
jokes.punchline: Mike's sarcastic comment about how it feels
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic callback to their earlier good feelings about donating.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, it feels great to give to charity. It really warms your heart. Of course, it also empties your wallet, but that's a small price to pay for feeling morally superior to everyone around you.

jokes.text: We've really been spoiling the children with all the food and warm clothing
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike asking for donation back
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about 'spoiling' kids with basic necessities
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcastic response that highlights the absurdity of taking money from needy children.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We'd like our donation back.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Yeah, we need it.

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, but once a donation is made, we can't return it.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, that's not very charitable of you.

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: The money goes directly to helping children in need - food, shelter, education.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you're spoiling them.

jokes.text: And I, and I spent my sweet 16 being chased around a tire yard by an...escaped mental patient, who, in his own words,'wanted to kill me' or whatever.
jokes.setup: Phoebe defending her right to a proper wedding
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of extremely traumatic birthday experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe backstory - traumatic but delivered with casual 'or whatever.' Excellent dark comedy with great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I want a real wedding! I want a dress and a cake and flowers and a real honeymoon! I didn't have a real birthday party until I was in my twenties because my mother was in jail.

jokes.text: She could've been talking about either one of us
jokes.setup: Phoebe calls someone a 'sweaty little man'
jokes.punchline: Mike's observation that it applies to both him and the charity worker
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor that shows Mike's realistic self-assessment.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You're a sweaty little man!

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Hey, that's not fair. I'm not sweaty.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You are too!

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, okay, I'm a little sweaty. But so is that charity guy over there.

jokes.text: We love kids Love'em to death Well, not actually to death. That's just a figure of speech
jokes.setup: Trying to impress adoption social worker
jokes.punchline: Chandler's unfortunate word choice and immediate correction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing how nerves can lead to poor word choices in important situations.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're very excited about having a black baby.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: An African American baby. Oh God, I'm gonna say something wrong, aren't I?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, no, you're doing great.

jokes.text: We love kids the appropriate amount As allowed by law
jokes.setup: Previous awkward comment about loving kids
jokes.punchline: Making it sound even more suspicious and clinical
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes their nervousness even worse. The legal phrasing is particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I love kids.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, that came out a little wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I love kids. I want to have kids.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's not better.

jokes.text: Because we should get a baby before them! That guy tried to sell me drugs!
jokes.setup: Social worker asks about other couples adopting
jokes.punchline: Monica's competitive nature and random accusation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Monica's competitiveness leading to desperate sabotage attempts.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: Have you considered that other couples might be adopting too?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? No! We're getting those babies!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And I don't appreciate you trying to make us compete with some couple from... from New Jersey or something!

jokes.text: But other than that, wholesome, wholesome building
jokes.setup: Just accused neighbor of drug dealing
jokes.punchline: Trying to salvage the building's reputation
jokes.explanation: Good damage control humor showing Monica's attempts to backtrack from her accusation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I just accused our neighbor of drug dealing! What if he sues us? What if the building gets condemned? What if nobody wants to live here anymore?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, relax. I'm sure it's fine.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Fine?! Chandler, we live in a building where people think there are drugs! We might as well be living in a crack house!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, what's wrong with that? I lived in a crack house once.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not helping, Joey!

jokes.text: Rat Bastard!
jokes.setup: Learning Joey didn't call the social worker back
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate harsh judgment
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat with good economy but predictable response.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You didn't call the social worker back? What is wrong with you? Oh, that's right, everything.

jokes.text: Oh, God, no! No, no, no, no. No! Nope...... No, no, no, no....
jokes.setup: Asked if they're friends with Joey
jokes.punchline: Monica's increasingly desperate denial
jokes.explanation: Good escalating denial that shows Monica's panic, though somewhat repetitive.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Friends with Joey? No, no, no. We're not... I mean, we barely know each other. He's just someone who lives here. I don't even like him that much, honestly. And he definitely doesn't like me. We have nothing in common. Nothing! We don't hang out, we don't talk, we don't... Why are you looking at me like that? Fine! Yes, okay, we're friends with Joey!

jokes.text: Hello Anybody in there ordered a celebrity?
jokes.setup: Joey arriving unexpectedly during adoption interview
jokes.punchline: His grandiose self-introduction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's earlier celebrity delusions, perfectly timed for maximum awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Joey Tribbiani. You may know me from such films as... well, actually, you probably don't know me from any films. But I'm an actor! And I'm very talented. And I'm very, very good-looking.

jokes.text: Oh, that's just some crazy guy who roams the halls here He's great with kids though
jokes.setup: Trying to explain away Joey to social worker
jokes.punchline: Claiming the 'crazy guy' is good with children
jokes.explanation: Good improvised damage control that ironically makes Joey sound more concerning.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's great with kids!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: He's a little crazy, but he's great with kids.

jokes.text: Oh, relax! I didn't say the F word.
jokes.setup: Ross says 'Son of a bitch' in front of baby
jokes.punchline: His defense focuses on a different inappropriate word
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Ross missing the point about inappropriate language around children.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Son of a bitch!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross! The baby!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? I didn't say the F-word!

jokes.text: I know what this is all about. You've always been jealous of my hair
jokes.setup: Ross wants Rachel to try the swing
jokes.punchline: Rachel's narcissistic interpretation of his motives
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's vanity and paranoia about her hair, connecting to earlier trauma.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Come on, try the swing. It'll be fun!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, so you want me to go on the swing so you can watch me and think about how pretty I look while I'm swinging?

jokes.text: If you hold the spider. What? Where? Where? "IF" you hold a spider.
jokes.setup: Rachel agrees to try swing if Ross holds a spider
jokes.punchline: Ross's immediate panic at just the word 'spider'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's spider phobia is just as irrational as Rachel's swing fear.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. I'll try the swing if you hold a spider.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A spider?! No, no, no. Absolutely not. I can't do that.

jokes.text: A.....doption!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to communicate through the door
jokes.punchline: His exaggerated pronunciation of 'adoption'
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment showing Joey's confused attempt to communicate.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A-DOP-SHUN!

jokes.text: Keep on roaming, Bert! We don't want any crazy today!
jokes.setup: Calling Joey 'Bert' the crazy hall-roamer
jokes.punchline: Monica's dismissive treatment of their 'friend'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the 'crazy hall-roamer' deception with Monica's authoritative dismissal.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Who's Bert?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The crazy guy in the hall!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, him. Yeah, he's not really our friend.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. He has a caretaker, his older brother. Ernie
jokes.setup: Explaining the 'crazy' Joey needs supervision
jokes.punchline: Naming his caretaker 'Ernie' (Bert and Ernie reference)
jokes.explanation: Excellent quick thinking that creates a Sesame Street reference, showing Chandler's wit under pressure.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey needs supervision.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're calling him Ernie.

jokes.text: Bert and Ernie? You can't make this stuff up!
jokes.setup: Chandler's improvised Bert and Ernie names
jokes.punchline: Social worker's comment about not making it up (when it's completely made up)
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic moment where the social worker appreciates the 'authenticity' of completely fabricated information.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Bert and Ernie? No, no, no. It's Gellir and Bing.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: Those aren't real names.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, we're not making them up!

jokes.text: Oh, it ended okay. One of my friends shot him
jokes.setup: Discussion of Phoebe's traumatic Sweet 16
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of friend shooting the mental patient
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Phoebe humor with incredible economy. The casual delivery of extreme violence is quintessential Phoebe.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: My Sweet 16 was terrible. My friend shot a mental patient.

jokes.text: And if that guy at the charity gives us a hard time, my friend hasn't shot anyone in a really~ long time.
jokes.setup: Previous mention of friend who shot someone
jokes.punchline: Implying the friend might be ready to shoot again
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that takes the dark humor further. The 'really long time' qualifier makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, so he's ready to shoot again?

jokes.text: Oh, by the way, you're more than welcome to look under any of the furniture because, believe me, you won't find any porn or cigarettes under there.
jokes.setup: Trying to impress social worker with cleanliness
jokes.punchline: Bringing up inappropriate items unprompted
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Monica's nervous over-compensation, though somewhat repetitive of earlier material.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Everything is clean. Spotless. I even organized the cabinets by color and food group.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: That's... very thorough.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And I want you to know, there are absolutely no dead bodies in the freezer.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: ...I wasn't going to check.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Or in the walls. The walls are clean too.

jokes.text: We don't have a code word! We don't? We really should
jokes.setup: Joey mentions 'Bert' as their code word for danger
jokes.punchline: Chandler's realization they don't have one, then immediate agreement they need one
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows how the couple gets distracted even during crisis situations.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, we don't have a code word?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We need a code word!

jokes.text: From now on, Bert will be our code word for danger.
jokes.setup: Agreeing they need a code word
jokes.punchline: Officially adopting 'Bert' during the middle of the emergency
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's need to organize even during chaos.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We need a code word. Something we can say if things get really bad during the birth.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't know. Something that means 'I can't take it anymore, get me drugs.'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How about 'Bert'?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Bert? Why Bert?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know, it just came to me.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. Bert it is.

jokes.text: Some people don't get him but I think he's really funny.
jokes.setup: Explaining away Joey's behavior to social worker
jokes.punchline: Chandler's assessment sounds like a TV critic reviewing Joey
jokes.explanation: Meta humor that could apply to audience reactions to Joey's character on the show.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, he's a complex character. Very physical. Sometimes he says things that don't quite make sense, but the audience seems to love him anyway. He's not the brightest, but he has a certain charm. And frankly, the show wouldn't work without him.

jokes.text: I forgot my bat.
jokes.setup: Joey finally leaving the adoption interview area
jokes.punchline: His random excuse to return
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist moment that shows Joey's lack of social awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! I forgot my... my... lucky adoption interviewing tie!

jokes.text: And we don't want to buy your bat!
jokes.setup: Joey mentioning his bat
jokes.punchline: Monica's assumption he's trying to sell it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that maintains the 'crazy hall-roamer' fiction with Monica's protective response.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I got a bat.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're selling it?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, I'm keeping it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Then why are you telling me?

jokes.text: Bert, Bert, Bert.
jokes.setup: Using their new code word for danger
jokes.punchline: Chandler's urgent repetition of 'Bert'
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to their newly established code word, showing it in action.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Bert! Bert! BERT!

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Probably you don't even remember my name! It's Joey, by the way. And don't bother telling me yours because I totally remember it Lady
jokes.setup: Confronting the social worker about not calling
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident claim to remember her name followed by calling her 'Lady'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment showing his complete lack of self-awareness. The escalation and reversal is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Of course I remember your name!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's... Lady.

jokes.text: No, don't try to turn this around on me, okay? I am not some kind of....social work, okay? that you can just....do
jokes.setup: Joey trying to make a point about being used
jokes.punchline: His mangled attempt at an analogy using 'social work'
jokes.explanation: Great Joey verbal confusion moment where he tries to make a metaphor but gets lost in his own words.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's like... it's like social work. You do it because you care, not because you're gonna get anything out of it.

jokes.text: You know how many women I had to sleep with to get over you?
jokes.setup: Joey claiming his heart was broken
jokes.punchline: His method of 'getting over' her
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey approach to relationships that's both cringey and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My heart was broken. So how did I get over her? I got under Chandler.

jokes.text: Joey, wait! No! I waited a long time. I can't wait any more
jokes.setup: Social worker trying to stop Joey from leaving
jokes.punchline: Joey's dramatic response that sounds like he's about to do something drastic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays with audience expectations about what Joey might do.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: Joey, you can't leave. We need to talk about this.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You don't understand. I have to go. This is something I need to do. And I may never come back.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: Joey, please—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm going to go be in a commercial.

jokes.text: Boy, you people are nice and I've got to say I think you're going to make excellent parents
jokes.setup: All the chaos and disasters during the interview
jokes.punchline: Social worker's positive assessment despite everything
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist that subverts expectations about the interview outcome.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: Well, despite the minor incidents today, I have to say I'm very impressed with your home.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: Yes. The love and support you all show each other is remarkable. I'm going to recommend you for adoption.

jokes.text: Are you here to take more money? Because I think what you're looking for is an ATM
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike returning to charity
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic assumption about their motives
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that shows the charity worker's justified cynicism.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We're back to volunteer at the charity again!

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: Oh, that's wonderful! Let me guess — you two finally figured out that tax write-offs are a thing?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: What? No, we just want to help people.

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: Sure, sure. And I'm guessing the matching volunteer t-shirts have nothing to do with your Instagram aesthetic?

jokes.text: On behalf of the children of New York, I reject your money
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Mike trying to give money back
jokes.punchline: Worker dramatically refusing their donation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic reversal with great economy. The formal phrasing makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Charity Worker
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I can't accept this.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Why not? It's a generous donation.

dialogue.speaker: Charity Worker
dialogue.line: Because it's blood money! You got it from... from selling your hair!

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: It's not blood money, it's just regular money.

dialogue.speaker: Charity Worker
dialogue.line: I don't care! The point is, I can't accept money from people who have compromised their morals!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: But we're trying to do good!

dialogue.speaker: Charity Worker
dialogue.line: No! The answer is no! And it will always be no!

jokes.text: And when you do, make sure you ask for Brian. Oh, is that you? No
jokes.setup: Charity worker recommending they ask for 'Brian' next time
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's not Brian despite giving the recommendation
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that adds a final twist to the charity worker's character.

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: Next time, just ask for Brian.

dialogue.speaker: Charity worker
dialogue.line: I'm Mike.

jokes.text: Have you seen Joey's bat?
jokes.setup: Previous mentions of Joey's bat
jokes.punchline: Someone else looking for the mysterious bat
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that maintains the bat running gag but doesn't add much new humor.

jokes.text: I got a spider There were two. I picked the bigger one
jokes.setup: Rachel facing her swing fear by having Ross face his spider fear
jokes.punchline: Her practical approach to spider selection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's practical approach even to Ross's fears.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: OK, so I found a spider for you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You found a spider? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: In the corner of the bathroom. It's huge!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I can't do this.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, come on. You said you'd face your fear.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, but a huge one? Can't we start with something smaller?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No! That's like me asking for a shorter swing. We're doing this.

jokes.text: You know what? I'm actually getting used to this little guy I don't really even feel him in here any more That's because it's on your neck
jokes.setup: Ross thinking he's getting comfortable with the spider
jokes.punchline: The spider has escaped and is on his neck
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect setup and payoff. Great physical comedy ending.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I got this. Just stay calm. The spider is contained, everything is fine...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait. Wait wait wait. Oh my God. OH MY GOD!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?! What happened?!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's on me! The spider is ON ME!

jokes.text: Ross!
jokes.setup: Spider revelation
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that provides the expected horrified response to the spider reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God! There's a spider in here!

tmdb_overview: Monica and Chandler nervously prepare for a visit from an adoption worker who will decide if they would be suitable parents. Meanwhile, Phoebe and Mike keep changing their minds about donating their wedding money to a children's charity. And Ross tries to help Rachel overcome her fear of swing sets so that baby Emma can enjoy the playground.
insight: Final season adoption episode leans on character comfort over fresh material.
